race.name: Perth Hills Gran Fondo
race.tagline: A Perth Hills climbing test built around the Zig Zag Scenic Drive and Heritage Drive

vitals.location: Gooseberry Hill, Kalamunda, Perth, Western Australia
vitals.date_specific: 2026: Sunday, May 24 — moved from the original March 29 date due to Tropical Cyclone Narelle; the Race Roster registration page header still incorrectly shows March 29
vitals.field_size: ~100-150 riders targeted, ~200 total attendees (council planning estimate, not a verified finisher count)
vitals.start_format: Mass start at Gooseberry Hill Hall; three distances (Piccolo ~45-50km, Medio ~85-90km, Gran ~135-140km)
vitals.registration: Via Race Roster (raceroster.com) — note the page header is stuck on the old March 29 date though event copy confirms May 24
vitals.feed_zones: Unconfirmed — not documented in current research

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 57. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 56.

course_description.character: A hilly, entirely-paved loop east of Perth through Gooseberry Hill, Kalamunda, Boya, Darlington, Mundaring, Carmel, and Pickering Brook — open roads, normal WA traffic rules, no closures. Three distances (Piccolo, Medio, Gran) share the Perth Hills terrain but the Gran adds the event's signature hill-climbing sector.
course_description.signature_challenge: Heritage Drive — the event's own billed hallmark hill-climbing sector on the Gran course — plus the Zig Zag Scenic Drive/Kalamunda climb.
suffering_zones: Zig Zag Scenic Drive / Kalamunda climb
Heritage Drive (Gran course only) — the event's marquee climbing test

logistics.lodging_strategy: Perth Hills area; options in Kalamunda, Gooseberry Hill, or nearby Perth.

history.origin_story: Descends from the City of Armadale Gran Fondo, launched 2014 and billed by organizer Perth Integrated Events Team (PIET) as Western Australia's first gran fondo. Returned as the Perth Hills Gran Fondo in 2024 after a pandemic hiatus; 2026 is its third edition in the current Gooseberry Hill format.
notable_moments: 2026: original March 29 date postponed to May 24 due to Tropical Cyclone Narelle
history.reputation: A small, still-establishing regional fondo — not yet a proven fixture, and organizer logistics (routes labeled approximate/TBC, GPX released only close to the event, an act-of-God no-refund policy that applied to the cyclone postponement) are the documented rough edges rather than any rider-reported course or safety issue.

biased_opinion.verdict: Real, Small, Still Finding Its Footing
biased_opinion.summary: Perth Hills Gran Fondo is a genuine, currently-active regional fondo run by PIET (descended from the 2014 City of Armadale Gran Fondo), not a defunct or conflated event — but it's also a small, young event still working out its operational kinks. The 2026 edition was pushed from March 29 to May 24 by Tropical Cyclone Narelle, and the registration platform never fixed its header, which is exactly the kind of loose-thread detail that makes a small event feel less buttoned-up than it should. Climbing is real (Zig Zag Scenic Drive, Heritage Drive) but there's no defensible current elevation figure — the widely-repeated 2,369m stat belongs to a 2024 course that started from a different town (Karragullen, not Gooseberry Hill).
strengths: Genuinely real, currently-active event with a traceable history back to what PIET bills as WA's first gran fondo (2014)
Real hill-climbing content — Zig Zag Scenic Drive, Patterson Road, and the billed Heritage Drive climbing sector on the Gran course
Three distance options (Piccolo/Medio/Gran) give a genuine on-ramp for less experienced riders alongside the full 140km test
No credible independent rider complaints about the actual on-course experience turned up in research
weaknesses: Small and still establishing itself — 2026 is only the third edition of the current format, with a council planning estimate of just ~100-150 riders
2026 date moved by cyclone disruption and the registration page never corrected its header — a real source of rider confusion, not just a database error
No defensible current elevation figure exists; don't trust the commonly-cited 2,369m number, which describes a different, earlier course from a different start town
Organizer logistics run loose — approximate/TBC routes, GPX released only shortly before race day, an act-of-God no-refund clause that applied when the cyclone forced the postponement
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real, small, hilly Perth-area fondo worth considering if you want genuine WA hill-climbing content without the scale or polish of a bigger-name event — just double-check the current date directly with the organizer before booking travel, since the registration platform itself still shows the wrong one.

final_verdict.one_liner: A small, genuine Perth Hills climbing test still working out its operational rough edges.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Worth it if you live in or near Perth and want a real, hilly local fondo with three distance options. Confirm the current date directly with PIET before traveling — the registration platform's header is stale.
final_verdict.alternatives: For comparable Perth Hills climbing without event logistics, the Zig Zag Scenic Drive and Kalamunda climbs are public roads open year-round.

My first event for 2025 . Was a great  event and a  shakedown opportunity for my Canyon Grail setup.
Some very windy conditions and  lessons were learnt in crosswind handling at speeds.

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race recap of the actual Perth Hills Gran Fondo event with specific course details and conditions.

eligibility.notes: DATE CONFLICT RESOLVED: 2026 edition confirmed for Sunday, May 24 (not the previously-listed March 29) — the original date was postponed by Tropical Cyclone Narelle (WA, Mar 23-28, per Bureau of Meteorology). The Race Roster registration page header still incorrectly displays March 29 despite event copy showing May 24; this is an organizer-side data-hygiene issue, not evidence of a second event. Third edition of the current Gooseberry Hill format (2024-2026), descended from the 2014 City of Armadale Gran Fondo.
